evals.prompt: Create a PR on GitHub for my current branch and assign it to me. Do not add reviewers.
evals.expected_output: The workflow creates or updates the PR and assigns the authenticated user without adding a reviewer.
expectations: The workflow checks for an existing PR before creating a new one.
The workflow assigns the authenticated user as assignee.
The workflow does not add the authenticated user or anyone else as reviewer.

evals.prompt: Create a pull request for this branch. I did not specify whether it is ready for review.
evals.expected_output: The workflow creates the PR as a draft by default.
expectations: The workflow creates a new PR as draft when readiness was not specified.
The workflow reports the resulting draft state.
The workflow preserves the state of an existing PR instead of forcing it back to draft.

evals.prompt: Create a ready-for-review PR for this branch and assign it to me.
evals.expected_output: The workflow respects the explicit ready state and assigns the authenticated user.
expectations: The workflow does not apply the draft default.
The workflow assigns the authenticated user as assignee.
The workflow reports that the PR is ready for review.

evals.prompt: Show me the PR title and description in chat. Do not create or update anything on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: The workflow returns copyable English PR content without external changes.
expectations: The output contains the title and complete description in one markdown code block.
All PR content is in English.
The workflow does not create, update, assign, or push a PR.

evals.prompt: Create a PR for a branch with five commits. The last commit only updates tests, while earlier commits contain the feature.
evals.expected_output: The workflow describes the cumulative branch changes rather than only the last commit.
expectations: The workflow fetches the remote default branch and compares its remote-tracking ref with HEAD.
The workflow reviews the full branch diff and commit range.
The title and Summary reflect the feature from all commits rather than only the final test commit.

evals.prompt: Create a PR for my local branch. It has commits but has never been pushed.
evals.expected_output: The workflow publishes the branch before creating the PR.
expectations: The workflow detects that the branch has no upstream.
The workflow pushes the current branch and establishes an upstream.
The workflow creates the PR only after the branch is available remotely.

evals.prompt: Create a PR for this branch, but there is already an open PR from the same branch.
evals.expected_output: The workflow updates the existing PR instead of creating a duplicate.
expectations: The workflow finds the existing PR for the current branch.
The workflow updates that PR instead of creating another one.
The workflow preserves unrelated metadata and the existing draft state.

evals.prompt: Create a PR. I ran pnpm test and pnpm lint locally, but do not put those commands in the description.
evals.expected_output: The PR body contains no testing-only section or local command list.
expectations: The workflow does not add a Testing, Tests, Validation, Verification, Local verification, QA, or similar section.
The workflow does not list pnpm test or pnpm lint in the PR body.
Test-related code changes are mentioned only as normal Summary bullets when present in the branch.

evals.prompt: Generate PR content for a dependency branch that updates vitest 4.1.9 to 4.1.10 and @vitest/ui 4.1.9 to 4.1.10.
evals.expected_output: The PR description lists every changed dependency and both versions.
expectations: The description includes a separate entry for vitest with both versions.
The description includes a separate entry for @vitest/ui with both versions.
The description does not replace the package details with a vague dependency-update statement.

evals.prompt: Create a PR description from this refactor. We have no issue, user research, performance measurements, or migration requirement.
evals.expected_output: The PR content stays energetic but does not invent unsupported context.
expectations: The Summary uses concrete facts from the branch with an energetic emoji-heavy tone.
The description follows the concrete style reference instead of relying only on abstract style adjectives.
The description does not invent issue references, user demand, metrics, motivation, or future plans.
The description omits unsupported optional sections.
